tier_definitions.tier_1_global: Global multidisciplinary firms (>5,000 staff worldwide) with Singapore offices doing site supervision as part of full-service offering
tier_definitions.tier_2_local: Singapore-headquartered or SEA-flagship full-service consultancies (200-16,000 staff) with strong local government track record
tier_definitions.tier_3_specialist: Singapore specialist supervision firms (50-150 staff) focused primarily on QP(S)/RE/RTO/QSS supervision contracts; PRIMARY competitive set for Elitez
tier_definitions.tier_4_body_shop: Smaller body shops (<50 staff) supplying RE/RTO labour to QP-led projects; lower brand and tooling sophistication
tier_definitions.adjacent: Regulators, accreditors, and industry associations that shape the market but do not directly compete

top_five.rationale: EPPU Grade 10 specialist with direct LTA mega-project wins (CRL Phase 1 Pkg 2 S$18.2M, LTA Contract 810C S$5.97M). Likely first-wave SAC/BCA licence applicant. The reference for what a successful, focused supervision firm looks like in Singapore — and the firm we are most often benchmarked against.

40+ year heritage with the most comprehensive multidisciplinary stack in Tier-3 (C&S + MEP + architecture + QS + environmental). Active LTA NSC, JRL and CRL pipeline. Owner-operator concentration risk under Vincent Ng (MD since 1997), but brand depth makes them hard to displace on legacy public-sector accounts.

Geotech-led with international tunnelling pedigree (Three Gorges, Delhi Metro). 90+ technical staff from 15 countries. Bundles QP(S) + RE + RTO in one shop. Buildings-side narrower than infra/geotech — gives us a pure-supervision pricing position they don't compete on.

MEP-led design+supervision with rare M&E specialism among Tier-3. 1980-founded, SEA reach (SG/VN/MY/MM). Buildings-tilted (residential/commercial/hospitality) — light on civil mega-projects, which lets us compete on the LTA / public-mega track without head-to-head.

Substructure specialist (1996-founded) with deep ERSS / deep excavation pedigree. Recently joined GISI Consulting Group (capital backing). Underground-focused niche means they win on deep-basement / Tuas Mega Port-class work but cede mainstream buildings supervision to us.

channel_partners.rationale: Tier-2 SG consultancy subsidiary (a major SJ-class incumbent) was a payroll-services customer of Elitez under a top-tier-firm-drafted framework agreement that ran a full annual cycle ending Q4 2024. SOLE site-supervision channel client to date; Elitez does not run parallel agreements with peer Tier-2 subsidiaries (RSP / Meinhardt / KTP / Beca) at present — re-engagement and parallel agreements are addressable space. The realized payroll-services margin from the completed engagement sets the FLOOR for Elitez's direct-to-developer S Pass billing post-2028 licence — never undercut. v4 §13 SJ-pushback risk reframes: a major Tier-2 has been a commercial customer, not an adversary; operators carry no SJ non-compete.

competitors.name: RCY Pte. Ltd.
competitors.hq: 1 Harvey Road, #05 Tan Heng Lee Building, Singapore 369610
competitors.pricing_range_published: Tier-3 firm rates: S$8,500–9,500 RTO / S$13,000–15,500 RE
competitors.primary_value_prop: EPPU Grade 10 QP(S) specialist with deep LTA infrastructure track record
strengths: Direct LTA mega-project wins (CRL Phase 1 Pkg 2, 810C)
S$4.5M paid-up capital signals tender-bonding credibility
Full service: QP(S), AC, periodic inspection, façade
weaknesses: No public-facing thought leadership / insights content
competitors.website_design_notes: Navy/white palette, professional typography, mobile-responsive, SSL valid. But aesthetic is 2010s-era; portfolio images lack captions; CSS unoptimised.

competitors.name: CKMBT International Pte. Ltd.
competitors.hq: 140 Paya Lebar Road, #09-09 AZ @ Paya Lebar, Singapore 409015
competitors.primary_value_prop: Cross-border ASEAN supervision firm with offices in Singapore, India, Malaysia
strengths: Underground depth via CRL and KVMRT
competitors.website_design_notes: Clean nav and clear service categorisation but dominated by placeholder graphics. Static feel. Looks under-maintained.

competitors.name: GeoAlliance Consultants Pte Ltd
competitors.hq: 33 Ubi Avenue 3, #05-11 Vertex Tower B, Singapore 408868
competitors.primary_value_prop: Geotech-led specialist with international tunnelling pedigree and full RE/RTO supply
strengths: Three Gorges / Delhi Metro on CV
Bundles QP(S) + RE + RTO in one shop
All four major SG infra clients (LTA/PUB/JTC/HDB)
weaknesses: Geotech-heavy may underweight pure C&S work
competitors.website_design_notes: Cleaner than peers — clear nav, organised sections, shows multinational team breadth. Dated aesthetic. Above Tier-3 median.

competitors.name: LYL Consultants Pte Ltd
competitors.primary_value_prop: Substructure specialist — design AND supervision for deep excavation and tunnel works
strengths: Recently joined GISI Consulting Group (capital backing)
International project list (HK, Dubai, Melbourne, Doha)
competitors.website_design_notes: Detailed project histories communicate expertise. Layout text-heavy, lacks modern hierarchy. Pre-2020s aesthetic. Mobile-responsive but dated.

competitors.name: RJ Crocker Consultants Pte Ltd
competitors.primary_value_prop: Most comprehensive Tier-3 — C&S + MEP + architecture + QS under one roof for QP(S) projects
strengths: One of THE top consultancies for QP Supervision in SG
weaknesses: Award logos as placeholders without citation
competitors.website_design_notes: Intuitive nav, prominent values display, project showcase. Logical hierarchy. Aesthetic somewhat dated. Solid mid-tier.

competitors.name: Rankine & Hill (Singapore) Pte Ltd
competitors.primary_value_prop: MEP-led design+supervision firm serving residential/commercial/hospitality across SEA
strengths: Strong M&E pedigree — rare among Tier-3
Early BIM adopter, Green Mark trained
weaknesses: Less infra/LTA pedigree than RJ Crocker / GeoAlliance
competitors.website_design_notes: Effective portfolio with anthology categorisation. Intuitive nav. Sparse on company background and team. Professional but content-thin.

competitors.name: Surbana Jurong (SJ Group)
competitors.hq: Surbana Jurong Campus, 8 CleanTech Loop, Singapore 637145
competitors.primary_value_prop: Temasek-linked SEA flagship — 16,000 staff, full lifecycle from masterplan to supervision.
strengths: Largest by far in scale — default for govt flagships
Full lifecycle: planning, design, supervision, FM
Holds the QP(S) sign-off and EPPU panel access end-clients buy from
weaknesses: High pricing relative to specialist Tier-3
Generalist — not specialists in pure QP(S)
competitors.website_design_notes: WebFetch blocked (403). Public profile: corporate-modern, group-level brand, polished but generic.

competitors.name: RSP Architects Planners & Engineers
competitors.primary_value_prop: Oldest Singapore architecture firm (1956) with engineering arm; iconic landmark portfolio
strengths: Architect-led — uncommon in QP(S) competitor set
weaknesses: Architecture-led, not supervision-led — QP(S) not core
Engineering via secondary Squire Mech brand
competitors.website_design_notes: Strong portfolio showcase, organised navigation, comprehensive global presence. Premium aesthetic supports market position.

competitors.name: Meinhardt Group (Singapore)
competitors.hq: 168 Jalan Bukit Merah, #09-01 Connection One, Singapore 150168
competitors.primary_value_prop: Global engineering consultancy with deep Singapore roots and full design+specialist+manage stack
weaknesses: QP(S) is one of many lines, not flagship
competitors.website_design_notes: Effective global navigation, hero carousel, testimonials. Dense menu, lacks project specifics on home. Corporate-standard.

competitors.name: KTP Consultants Pte Ltd (SJ Group)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Multi-disciplinary engineering with prefab MEP/PPVC innovation; SJ Group brand backing
strengths: Joined Surbana Jurong 2015 — capital + brand halo
Senior REs with Building Control Act knowledge
weaknesses: Brand subordinate to SJ Group post-acquisition
Full-service positioning dilutes pure-play supervision pitch
competitors.website_design_notes: WebFetch blocked. Public references suggest corporate-engineering-firm aesthetic similar to Meinhardt.

competitors.name: Beca (Singapore)
competitors.primary_value_prop: NZ-origin engineering consultancy with strong APAC sustainability and smart-buildings positioning
competitors.website_design_notes: Clean layout, strong visual hierarchy. Limited Singapore-specific detail given the .com domain shared with global.

competitors.name: WSP (Singapore)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Global engineering powerhouse (~70k staff) with Singapore as Asia regional hub
weaknesses: Premium global pricing not competitive vs Tier-3 on small jobs
competitors.website_design_notes: WebFetch blocked. Public profile: WSP global brand is polished modern blue/white, mobile-responsive, corporate-confident.

competitors.name: AECOM Singapore Pte Ltd
competitors.primary_value_prop: Multidisciplinary global delivery — water, transport, geotech under one roof
strengths: Same building as WSP — dense talent cluster
competitors.website_design_notes: Modern AECOM global template, blue/black palette, clean Singapore subsite. Mobile-responsive, SSL valid. Corporate but polished.

competitors.name: Arup Singapore
competitors.primary_value_prop: Premier global design engineer with deep specialist services (façade, fire, acoustics) and 50+ year SG tenure
strengths: 350+ SG staff with rare specialist disciplines
Trust-led brand carries weight on submissions
Sustainability and digital ahead of peers
weaknesses: Premium pricing — prices itself out of pure supervision RFPs
Brand prestige attracts only flagship work
competitors.website_design_notes: Best-in-class for engineering: typographically refined, image-led case studies, fast, accessible, mobile-perfect.

competitors.name: Mott MacDonald Singapore Pte Ltd
competitors.primary_value_prop: UK-origin employee-owned engineering firm; ~300 SG staff with deep rail/tunnel pedigree
strengths: Very strong rail/tunnel reputation — LTA fit
weaknesses: Less brand recognition with private SG developers
competitors.website_design_notes: Clean, content-rich, well-structured. Strong sector navigation. Mobile-responsive.

competitors.name: AtkinsRealis Singapore
competitors.primary_value_prop: Anglo-Canadian engineering powerhouse (~37k global) with SEA hub in Singapore
strengths: EPC supervision capability beyond just QP(S)
weaknesses: Brand still settling post-rebrand from SNC-Lavalin
competitors.website_design_notes: Modern global rebrand site (post-SNC-Lavalin). Clean, dark+orange palette, corporate-confident.

competitors.name: LEM Consultants
competitors.hq: 71C Sultan Gate, Sultan Arts Village, Singapore 198496
competitors.primary_value_prop: Small-shop labour supplier of PE/RE/RTO bodies for niche supervision
weaknesses: Tiny scale — cannot bid LTA mega-projects
competitors.website_design_notes: Functional but riddled with typos and placeholder text. BizSafe and BCA logos shown but content quality undercuts credibility.

competitors.name: JJGL Ventures Pte Ltd
competitors.primary_value_prop: Body-shop RTO supplier for major construction supervision (8+ year experience requirement)
competitors.website_design_notes: No corporate website found — presence limited to job boards (MyCareersFuture, foundit).

competitors.name: Wide Wings Pte Ltd
competitors.primary_value_prop: Young body-shop targeting LTA viaduct/MRT supervision niches
strengths: BIM Manager hiring signals digital posture
competitors.website_design_notes: No corporate website discoverable. Presence limited to job boards.

competitors.name: Syntech E&C Pte Ltd
competitors.primary_value_prop: New (2023) general contractor entity with site supervision support work
strengths: Backed by Syntech Engineers (1986 — 30yr automotive/industrial heritage)
weaknesses: Parent's website is for a different (vehicle) business
Site supervision is secondary, not flagship
competitors.website_design_notes: Parent Syntech Engineers site has clear nav, professional branding. But content for trucks/CCTV — brand context for E&C is weak.

competitors.name: BCA Building Engineering Group
competitors.primary_value_prop: Singapore's site-supervision regulator — driving the 2028 firm-licensing inflection
strengths: Statutory authority — all firms regulated by them
competitors.website_design_notes: SGDI / .gov.sg standard government site — functional, accessible, plain. Not a competitor; included as market shaper.

competitors.name: Institution of Engineers Singapore (IES)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Premier engineering professional body — co-accreditor for all SG site supervisors
weaknesses: Website has duplicate navigation and placeholder SVGs
competitors.website_design_notes: Clear hierarchical nav for a 60-year institution. Strengths offset by duplicate menus and placeholder image elements.

competitors.name: Association of Consulting Engineers Singapore (ACES)
competitors.hq: 18 Sin Ming Lane, #06-01 Midview City, Singapore 573960
competitors.primary_value_prop: Trade body for consulting engineering firm directors/partners — shapes pricing norms and accreditation
strengths: Tie to firm-level (not individual) accreditation — relevant to 2028
Member directories for C&S, M&E, specialised registries
competitors.website_design_notes: Dated visual design, limited responsive features, sparse imagery, no clear CTAs. Functional informational hub. HTTP root.
